Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. LLM Architecture Gallery 190 upvotes, 11 comments
  2. In Memoriam: John W. Addison, my PhD advisor 80 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 305 upvotes, 29 comments
  4. Kangina 37 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. Learning athletic humanoid tennis skills from imperfect human motion data 112 upvotes, 21 comments
  6. Animated 'Firefly' Reboot in Development from Nathan Fillion, 20th TV 146 upvotes, 31 comments
  7. Canada's bill C-22 mandates mass metadata surveillance of Canadians 144 upvotes, 35 comments
  8. Nasdaq's Shame 63 upvotes, 13 comments
  9. Excel incorrectly assumes that the year 1900 is a leap year 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  10. A new Bigfoot documentary helps explain our conspiracy-minded era 19 upvotes, 2 comments
  11. Show HN: Signet – Autonomous wildfire tracking from satellite and weather data 103 upvotes, 28 comments
  12. Show HN: GDSL – 800 line kernel: Lisp subset in 500, C subset in 1300 52 upvotes, 13 comments
  13. Show HN: Open-source playground to red-team AI agents with exploits published 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  14. //go:fix inline and the source-level inliner 94 upvotes, 32 comments
  15. Bus travel from Lima to Rio de Janeiro 107 upvotes, 40 comments
  16. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 196 upvotes, 85 comments
  17. Show HN: What if your synthesizer was powered by APL (or a dumb K clone)? 74 upvotes, 28 comments
  18. Chrome DevTools MCP 268 upvotes, 127 comments
  19. Glassworm Is Back: A New Wave of Invisible Unicode Attacks Hits Repositories 202 upvotes, 118 comments
  20. C++26: The Oxford Variadic Comma 99 upvotes, 54 comments
  21. The 49MB web page 227 upvotes, 141 comments
  22. Grandparents are glued to their phones [video] 181 upvotes, 118 comments
  23. Stop Sloppypasta 64 upvotes, 35 comments
  24. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 169 upvotes, 113 comments
  25. Autoresearch Hub 30 upvotes, 15 comments
  26. LLMs can be exhausting 20 upvotes, 9 comments
  27. Type systems are leaky abstractions: the case of Map.take!/2 9 upvotes, 3 comments
  28. Ask HN: How is AI-assisted coding going for you professionally? 207 upvotes, 348 comments
  29. Show HN: Free OpenAI API Access with ChatGPT Account 8 upvotes, 7 comments
  30. Hollywood Enters Oscars Weekend in Existential Crisis 115 upvotes, 369 comments

Last generated 8:00am, 16 March 2026. Source here.
